article.headline: Trammel's layup, block clinch Big Sky for Grizzlies
article.linkText: Trammel's late-game heroics give Montana Big Sky
article.story:
BOZEMAN, Mont. (AP) -- Dan Trammel saved his best for last for
the second game in a row.

	   Trammel sank a go-ahead layup as the shot clock expired and
blocked a layup at the other end as Montana beat Eastern Washington
70-66 Saturday to win the Big Sky tournament.

	   Montana heads to the NCAA tournament for the fifth time, but
first since 1997.

	   Trammel, who did a back flip when he was selected tourney MVP,
put Montana (16-14) up 67-66 with his layup with 1:51 remaining.

	   Eastern (17-13) had two chances to take the lead, but Marc
Axton's 3-point try was short, and Trammel swatted Jason Lewis'
shot in the lane with 25 seconds left.

	   "It was either he makes it or I block it," said Trammel, who
scored 14 points. "It was a little scary, but I was putting
everything on the line."

	   Lewis said: "The shot clock was running down and I went for it.
Trammel just come out of nowhere and swatted it."

	   In a semifinal Friday, Trammel slammed home a teammate's missed
3-pointer with 1.2 seconds left to give the Grizzlies a 70-68 win
over Montana State.

	   "I don't think anybody thought we could win coming in,"
Montana coach Don Holst said. "But we had a chance to win and we
did."

	   The Grizzlies could have made it easier on themselves, but
missed half their free throws down the stretch. Shane Christensen
hit one of two at the line with 23.6 seconds remaining to give
Montana a 68-66 lead.

	   Chris Hester mishandled an alley-oop pass on the Eagles' next
possession, and Eastern Washington was forced to foul. Travis
Greenwalt hit one of two free throws with 14.7 seconds left to put
Montana up 69-66, and Ryan Slider hit the second of two attempts
with 6.7 seconds left.

	   David Bell, the Grizzlies' top 3-point threat, scored most of
his game-high 16 points on slashing drives to the basket. Teammates
Brent Cummings and Christensen each had 10.

	   "They've seen me do a lot of shooting," Bell said. "I had to
show them the rest of my game."

	   T.J. Williams led Eastern Washington with 13 points. Alvin Snow
had 12, and Hester 10.

	   The game was tied five times in the first half. Axton's
3-pointer with 5:13 left tied it 30-30 and started a 10-3 run by
the Eagles, who ended the half with a 37-33 lead.

	   Eastern Washington led by as much as 57-52 with 9:32 left
following two free throws by Lewis.
